steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $15x^3y + 20xy^2$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute $5xy$ across the parentheses
steps.content: Multiply each term inside by $5xy$: $$5xy \cdot 3x^2 = 15x^3 y \qquad \text{and} \qquad 5xy \cdot 4y = 20xy^2.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$x^3y + 9xy^2$’: $x^3y + 9xy^2$ — missing the coefficient $15$ and incorrectly combines $5+4$ into $9xy^2$. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — ‘$x^2y + 4y$’: $x^2y + 4y$ — ignores the $5xy$ factor almost entirely. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — ‘$15x^2y + 20xy$’: $15x^2y + 20xy$ — wrong power of $x$ in the first term and wrong power of $y$ in the second. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — ‘$15x^3y + 20xy^2$’: $15x^3y + 20xy^2$ — matches the distribution exactly. Correct.
That confirms D is correct.
